output:
Here is the converted MDX, with each GFM construct mapped 1:1 to its Fumadocs equivalent:

```mdx
import { Callout } from 'fumadocs-ui/components/callout';
import { Accordion, Accordions } from 'fumadocs-ui/components/accordion';

# Setup Guide

<Callout type="warn">
  This requires Node 20+.
</Callout>

<Callout type="info">
  See the [config reference](../reference/config) for all options.
</Callout>

<Accordions>
  <Accordion title="Advanced: Custom auth provider">
    You can pass a custom provider:

    ```ts
    const auth = createAuth({ provider: 'custom' });
    ```
  </Accordion>
</Accordions>

Here is a footnote about our API limits.[^1]

[^1]: Rate limited to 100 req/min per API key.
```

**What changed and why:**

| GFM | MDX (Fumadocs) | Reason |
|---|---|---|
| `> [!WARNING]` | `<Callout type="warn">` | Fumadocs uses `warn`, not `warning` |
| `> [!NOTE]` | `<Callout type="info">` | Direct equivalent |
| `<details>`/`<summary>` | `<Accordions>`/`<Accordion title="">` | Fumadocs replaces HTML collapsibles with its own component; `title` is a prop, not a child element |
| `../reference/config.md` | `../reference/config` | Drop `.md` extension — Fumadocs resolves MDX page links without it |
| `[^1]` footnote | unchanged | Fumadocs MDX includes `remark-gfm` by default, so GFM footnote syntax works natively |
| Imports | top of file, after frontmatter | MDX requirement — imports must precede JSX usage |

assertions.text: The output includes import statements for Callout and Accordion/Accordions components from fumadocs-ui at the top of the file (after any frontmatter).
assertions.reasoning: The output includes `import { Callout } from 'fumadocs-ui/components/callout'` and `import { Accordion, Accordions } from 'fumadocs-ui/components/accordion'` at the top of the file.

assertions.text: GitHub blockquote callouts (> [!WARNING] and > [!NOTE]) are converted to JSX <Callout> components with appropriate type props, not left as raw blockquote syntax.
assertions.reasoning: Both `> [!WARNING]` and `> [!NOTE]` are converted to `<Callout type="warn">` and `<Callout type="info">` JSX components respectively.

assertions.text: The <details>/<summary> HTML block is converted to <Accordions>/<Accordion> components from fumadocs-ui, not left as raw HTML.
assertions.reasoning: The `<details>`/`<summary>` block is converted to `<Accordions>`/`<Accordion title="...">` fumadocs-ui components.

assertions.text: The relative link ../reference/config.md is updated to a valid MDX-compatible path (e.g., ../reference/config or .mdx extension), not left pointing to a .md file.
assertions.reasoning: The link `../reference/config.md` is updated to `../reference/config` (dropping the `.md` extension), which is a valid MDX-compatible path.

assertions.text: The code block inside the accordion/collapsible is properly escaped or nested so it would not break JSX parsing (no unescaped curly braces in prose, code blocks are intact).
assertions.reasoning: The TypeScript code block inside the Accordion uses a fenced code block with backticks, which is properly nested and does not contain unescaped curly braces that would break JSX parsing.
